name: nmap
description: Network scanner — port discovery, service/OS detection, NSE vulnerability scripts

name: masscan
description: Fastest port scanner — millions of packets/sec for subnet-wide sweeps

name: aircrack-ng
description: WPA/WPA2 handshake cracker — dictionary and brute force attacks on captured handshakes

description: WiFi network scanner — captures APs, clients, signal strength, handshakes

name: aireplay-ng
description: WiFi injection — deauth, fake auth, ARP replay to force handshake captures

name: kismet
description: Wireless IDS and passive scanner — WiFi, BT, Zigbee, SDR with REST API

name: hashcat
description: GPU-accelerated password cracker — WPA, NTLM, MD5, SHA, bcrypt, and 300+ hash types

name: john
description: CPU password cracker — flexible format support, custom rules, good for shadow files

name: hydra
description: Online brute forcer — SSH, FTP, HTTP, SMB, RDP, MySQL login cracking

name: metasploit
description: Exploitation framework — 2000+ exploits, payloads, post-exploitation, pivoting

name: bettercap
description: Network attack toolkit — ARP spoof, DNS spoof, HTTPS proxy, WiFi deauth, BLE recon

name: ettercap
description: MITM attack suite — ARP poisoning, DNS spoofing, credential sniffing

name: responder
description: LLMNR/NBT-NS/MDNS poisoner — captures NTLMv2 hashes from Windows name resolution

name: wireshark
description: Packet analyzer GUI — deep protocol inspection for any capture file

name: tshark
description: CLI packet analyzer — extract fields, filter protocols, process captures in scripts

name: tcpdump
description: Low-level packet capture — lightweight, good for long-running captures and feeding other tools

name: p0f
description: Passive OS fingerprinting — identifies OS from TCP SYN packets without sending anything

name: nikto
description: Web server scanner — misconfigs, default files, outdated versions, dangerous methods

description: WPS attack tool — Pixie Dust and brute force, alternative to reaver

description: Automated WiFi auditor — WPA, WPS, WEP attacks with minimal interaction

name: WF01-wpa-handshake-crack
description: Capture WPA handshake via deauth then crack with wordlist

name: WF02-pmkid-clientless
description: Extract PMKID without needing a connected client — passive WPA attack

name: WF05-arp-mitm
description: ARP spoof MITM — intercept traffic between target and gateway

name: WF06-llmnr-poison
description: Poison LLMNR/NBT-NS to capture NTLMv2 hashes from Windows machines

name: WF07-eternalblue
description: MS17-010 EternalBlue SMB exploit — remote code execution on unpatched Windows

name: WF19-full-network-compromise
description: Full network penetration — recon to domain admin via chained attacks

name: WF03-wps-pixie-bruteforce
description: Exploit WPS via Pixie Dust or brute force to recover WPA password without handshake
steps: Scan for WPS-enabled APs with wash

name: WF04-evil-twin-credential-harvest
description: Clone target AP, deauth clients, serve captive portal to harvest WiFi credentials
steps: Wait for auto-connect to evil twin
Serve captive portal to harvest credentials

name: WF08-web-sqli-exfil
description: Scan web service for SQL injection, enumerate databases, and exfiltrate data
steps: Use sqlmap to find injectable params

name: WF09-service-bruteforce
description: Discover and brute force SSH, RDP, HTTP, FTP using default or dictionary credentials

name: WF10-ble-recon-gatt-extract
description: Discover BLE devices, enumerate GATT services, read characteristics to extract data

name: WF11-smb-relay-attack
description: Poison LLMNR/NBT-NS and relay NTLM auth to SMB targets for shell access without cracking

name: WF12-default-credential-spray
description: Identify vendor/model of network devices and spray factory-default credentials

name: WF13-hidden-ssid-discovery
description: Discover hidden SSIDs by deauthing clients to force probe requests, then crack

name: WF14-wpad-poisoning
description: Poison WPAD LLMNR queries to become HTTP proxy and harvest credentials from Windows hosts

name: WF15-dns-spoof-phishing
description: ARP spoof into MITM, poison DNS to redirect victims to phishing portal

name: WF16-detect-arp-spoof
description: Baseline ARP tables, monitor with arpwatch, detect and mitigate ARP spoofing

name: WF17-detect-bruteforce-ban
description: Configure fail2ban to detect brute force patterns and auto-ban offending IPs

name: WF18-detect-rogue-ap
description: Passively scan beacons, build known-good baseline, alert on rogue or evil twin APs

name: WF20-iot-device-exploitation
description: Discover IoT devices, fingerprint vendor, spray default creds, exploit CVEs
